ground_truth:
Patient Name: John Smith
DOB: 15/03/1965
Date: 10/01/2024

CARDIOLOGY CONSULTATION

Chief Complaint: Chest pain on exertion for 2 weeks.

History of Present Illness:
A 58-year-old male presenting with substernal chest pain, grade 3/10, occurring with physical activity. Pain radiates to left arm. No associated dyspnea, diaphoresis, or syncope.

Past Medical History:
- Hypertension, diagnosed 2018
- Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, diagnosed 2020
- Hyperlipidemia

Medications:
- Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Lisinopril 10mg once daily
- Atorvastatin 20mg at bedtime

Physical Examination:
BP: 145/92 mmHg | HR: 78 bpm | RR: 16/min
CVS: S1, S2 normal. No murmur or gallop.
Respiratory: Clear bilateral breath sounds.

Assessment:
1. Stable angina pectoris (Canadian Cardiovascular Society Class II)
2. Hypertension (Stage 1)
3. Type 2 DM with good glycemic control

Plan:
- ECG and echocardiogram ordered
- Treadmill stress test scheduled
- Titrate Lisinopril to 20mg
- Add Aspirin 81mg daily
- Follow-up in 2 weeks

Dr. Sarah Johnson, MD, FACC
Cardiology Department
